articles.title: Weather Tomorrow June 26: IMD warns of heavy rain in Delhi, Haryana, Punjab, Bihar, Bengal and Odisha; thunderstorm warning for Rajasthan, heatwave in East Uttar Pradesh
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Odisha
articles.full_text:
While residents of Delhi, Haryana, Punjab, Rajasthan, Bihar, West Bengal, Odisha, Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra, Karnataka, Telangana and Kerala are likely to see rain-related activity, some parts of eastern Uttar Pradesh, Bihar and Jharkhand may continue to experience heatwave conditions.

Delhi, Haryana and Punjab Weather Tomorrow
According to the IMD, Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi and Punjab are likely to experience isolated rainfall along with thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds.Wind speeds may reach 40-50 kmph, gusting up to 60 kmph, bringing temporary relief from the prevailing heat. Localized showers are expected to continue across parts of the region through the weekend.

Reports also suggest that Delhi may continue to witness cloudy skies and intermittent rainfall activity as monsoon conditions gradually strengthen.
Rajasthan Weather Tomorrow
Residents of West Rajasthan and East Rajasthan should prepare for thunderstorms and strong winds on June 26.The IMD has forecast:
Uttar Pradesh Weather Tomorrow
Weather conditions will vary across Uttar Pradesh.East Uttar Pradesh
- Severe heatwave conditions remain a concern in certain districts
West Uttar Pradesh
- Scattered rainfall expected
- More rain activity likely toward the end of the month
Bihar and Jharkhand Weather Tomorrow
Bihar remains under a dual weather threat.The IMD has forecast:
Thundersquall activity with wind speeds reaching 50-60 kmph, gusting up to 70 kmph, is also possible in isolated areas.
West Bengal Weather Tomorrow
Weather officials have indicated that Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim are likely to continue receiving widespread rainfall.In Kolkata and surrounding areas, cloudy skies and periodic rain are expected to continue over the coming days.
The IMD has warned of:
Odisha Weather Tomorrow
Odisha is likely to experience:- Scattered rainfall
Madhya Pradesh Weather Tomorrow
Both East Madhya Pradesh and West Madhya Pradesh are expected to receive scattered rainfall.The IMD has also forecast:
Maharashtra, Goa and Gujarat Weather Tomorrow
Konkan and Goa
Heavy rainfall is likely to continue, with some areas receiving intense monsoon showers.Madhya Maharashtra
Rainfall activity is expected along with thunderstorms.Gujarat
Scattered rainfall is likely across several districts as monsoon conditions remain active.Karnataka, Kerala and Telangana Weather Tomorrow
Southern states are expected to remain under active monsoon influence.Karnataka
Kerala
Telangana
Northeast India Weather Tomorrow
These states are likely to witness widespread rainfall on June 26:- Arunachal Pradesh
Heavy rainfall warnings remain in place for several northeastern districts, with isolated areas likely to receive very heavy showers.
IMD advisory for residents
The overall weather pattern on June 26 indicates active monsoon conditions across large parts of India. While northern and eastern states may experience thunderstorms and gusty winds, southern and western regions are expected to continue receiving significant rainfall.Residents in areas under heavy rain, thunderstorm and wind alerts are advised to monitor local weather updates and follow advisories issued by authorities as conditions may change rapidly throughout the day.

articles.title: Schools closed news: All CBSE, ICSE, private, govt schools in Delhi, Noida, Gurugram, UP, Rajasthan, MP, Chhattisgarh, Mumbai, Telangana, Odisha to resume classes from ...; Check state-wise reopening dates - news24online.com
articles.full_text:
Schools closed news: All CBSE, ICSE, private, govt schools in Delhi, Noida, Gurugram, UP, Rajasthan, MP, Chhattisgarh, Mumbai, Telangana, Odisha to resume classes from …; Check state-wise reopening dates
As summer vacations come to an end in most parts of the country, schools are preparing to reopen for the new academic session. However, a few states have extended holidays due to ongoing heatwave conditions. The reopening schedule differs across states and education boards. Here's a state-wise list of when students are expected to return to school.
As summer vacations come to an end in most parts of the country, schools are preparing to reopen for the new academic session. However, a few states have extended holidays due to ongoing heatwave conditions. The reopening schedule differs across states and education boards. Here's a state-wise list of when students are expected to return to school.
Uttar Pradesh, Delhi, Haryana and Punjab
Schools in Uttar Pradesh observed summer vacations from May 20 to June 15, 2026, and classes resumed on June 16. In Delhi, schools are closed for summer holidays from May 11 to June 30 and are scheduled to reopen on July 1. Haryana schools remained closed between May 25 and May 30 for summer break. In Punjab, schools are observing summer vacations from June 1 to June 30, with classes set to restart on July 1.
Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Bihar, Odisha and West Bengal
Madhya Pradesh schools remained closed for summer holidays from May 1 to June 15. In Chhattisgarh, the vacation period lasted from April 20 to June 15, and schools reopened on June 16. Authorities in several districts have also revised school timings due to intense heat conditions. Bihar schools observed summer holidays from June 1 to June 20 and resumed classes on June 21. Odisha began its summer break on April 27, while the reopening date is yet to be announced. In West Bengal, the summer vacation was extended until May 31, and schools reopened on June 1.
Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu and Kerala
Schools in Telangana and Andhra Pradesh remained closed for summer vacation from April 24 to June 11, with classes resuming thereafter. In Tamil Nadu, schools observed holidays from mid-April until June 4. Kerala has announced summer vacations for 2026, though the detailed schedule is still awaited from the state authorities.
Rajasthan and Jammu & Kashmir
Rajasthan schools remained closed from May 17 to June 20 for summer vacations and are scheduled to reopen on June 21. In Jammu & Kashmir, summer holidays will be observed during June and July, though the exact dates vary depending on the region.
Kendriya Vidyalaya Schools
Several Kendriya Vidyalaya regions, including Agra, Delhi, Lucknow, Patna, Ranchi, Kolkata, Chandigarh, Dehradun and Jammu, observed summer vacations from May 8 to June 16. Schools in these regions reopened on June 17, 2026.
Mumbai schools closed?
As of now, no official announcement has been made regarding a holiday for schools or colleges in Mumbai tomorrow. Students and parents are advised to stay updated through notifications from the Mumbai administration, the Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC), and their respective educational institutions. If weather conditions deteriorate further, authorities may review the situation and issue a fresh advisory. Until any such announcement is made, schools and colleges will continue to operate as per their regular schedule.
Weather May Affect School Reopening Dates
Authorities are closely monitoring weather conditions across the country. If temperatures rise further or heatwave conditions continue, some states may extend holidays or revise school timings to ensure the safety of students and staff.
